summary: The masseter muscle generates superior and lateral forces but does not cause significant posterior retraction. Rather, post-fracture retraction occurs mainly through actions of the mylohyoid and anterior bellies of digastrics pulling the anterior fragment backward.
formatted_summary: agent2 (Round 2 summarized): The masseter muscle generates superior and lateral forces but does not cause significant posterior retraction. Rather, post-fracture retraction occurs mainly through actions of the mylohyoid and anterior bellies of digastrics pulling the anterior fragment backward.. Answer choice: B
